name: decode_lz4
description:
Decodes the `value` (an lz4 string) into its original string. `buf_size` is the size of the buffer to decode into, this must be equal to or larger than the uncompressed size.
If `prepended_size` is set to `true`, it expects the original uncompressed size to be prepended to the compressed data.
`prepended_size` is useful for some implementations of lz4 that require the original size to be known before decoding.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: The lz4 block data to decode.

arguments.name: buf_size
arguments.description: The size of the buffer to decode into, this must be equal to or larger than the uncompressed size.

arguments.name: prepended_size
arguments.description: Some implementations of lz4 require the original uncompressed size to be prepended to the compressed data.

internal_failure_reasons: `value` unable to decode value with lz4 frame decoder.
`value` unable to decode value with lz4 block decoder.
`value` unable to decode because the output is too large for the buffer.
`value` unable to decode because the prepended size is not a valid integer.

examples.title: Decode Lz4 data with prepended size.
examples.return: The quick brown fox jumps over 13 lazy dogs.

examples.title: Decode Lz4 data without prepended size.
examples.return: The quick brown fox jumps over 13 lazy dogs.
